title: The Peel and the Pulse
lines: You press your palm against the loose wallpaper. It doesn't feel like paper; it feels like... skin.
A faint, rhythmic thrum vibrates beneath your touch. The wall is breathing, or something behind it is.
A thin, translucent fluid begins to weep from the tears in the wallpaper. It smells like old copper.

options.desc: 1. Pull a large strip of the paper away
2. Stare into the dark gaps and wait
3. Stumble back into the main attic
